_comment_top: R6 Pillar 6b / D-C-07 / task 056 — seed row for the CloseWorkspaceTabHandler. Surfaces a single LLM-visible 'close_workspace_tab(tabId)' function that closes a tab from the user's active workspace via IWorkspaceStateService.CloseTabAsync. Pinned tabs are refused with a structured response (the user must explicitly unpin first). Idempotent: closing an absent tab returns success.
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'CloseWorkspaceTabHandler' (= nameof handler class). Auto-discovered via ToolFrameworkExtensions.AddToolHandlersFromAssembly (ADR-010) — ZERO new top-level DI line.
_comment_persistence: Q4 hybrid persistence (R6 Pillar 6a): CloseTabAsync removes the Redis hot-tier row ONLY. Cosmos durable rows for previously-pinned data are PRESERVED per spec FR-32. This row's description echoes that contract so the LLM understands it cannot use this tool to delete pinned matter-attached data.
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = null (intentional). Workspace tabs are a chat-session UI affordance; every chat session has a workspace, so the tool is always available. Tenant isolation is enforced INSIDE IWorkspaceStateService (Redis key + Cosmos partition key include tenantId per NFR-16).
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool. The pin-state guard is mentioned so the LLM understands the refusal-on-pinned response is expected behavior (not an error). The idempotency note is included so the LLM does not re-prompt on stale-handle scenarios.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass with sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Closes an open workspace tab in the user's current chat-session workspace. Use this when the user asks to close a tab, dismiss a panel, or clear something from their workspace. Pass the stable 'tabId' of the tab. Pinned tabs cannot be closed through this tool — the response will indicate the tab is pinned and the user must explicitly unpin it first. Closing a non-existent or already-closed tab returns success (idempotent). Cosmos durable rows for previously-pinned data are preserved when a tab is closed.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat only. Workspace tabs are a chat-session concept; the playbook orchestrator has no workspace concept and the production node executors per NFR-08 do not include a tab-mutation executor.

sprk_jsonschema.title: CloseWorkspaceTabHandler Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of close_workspace_tab. 'tabId' is the stable identifier of the tab to close (matches WorkspaceTab.id from the prior turn's workspace-state snapshot).

tabId.description: Stable identifier of the workspace tab to close. Matches WorkspaceTab.id from the workspace-state block in the system prompt.

sprk_configuration._comment: No method-discriminator dispatch — this row exposes a single LLM-facing function. Empty object reserved for future per-row defaults (e.g., per-tenant audit-tag forwarding).
